svg.text: Where does element [1, 2, 3] live in flat memory?
Shape [2, 3, 4] — axis 0 size 2 (purple) · axis 1 size 3 (teal) · axis 2 size 4 (amber)
the number printed inside every cell below is that cell’s OWN flat-buffer offset (not a stored value). So the cell at position [1, 2, 3] showing ‘23’ means: ‘flat offset 23’.
1. The logical 3-D view — shape [2, 3, 4] = two 3×4 slabs
2. The flat buffer — Float64Array(24) — same 24 numbers, single row in memory
3. The formula — add up (index along each axis) × (its stride)
index along axis 0 × stride[0] + index along axis 1 × stride[1] + index along axis 2 × stride[2]
“skip 1 full axis-0 slab (12 cells), then 2 axis-1 rows (8 cells), then 3 axis-2 steps (3 cells).”
Strides come from the shape: stride[i] = product of shape[i+1..end]. For [2,3,4] that is 12, 4, 1.
